channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: FBI investigating New Year's Day attack in New Orleans that killed at least 15 people
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The FBI is investigating an early Wednesday attack in which a U.S. Army veteran drove a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year’s revelers in New Orleans, killing 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 10 people are wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ten people were wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub while they were waiting to get into a private event, police said. About 15 people were standing outside Amazura nightclub at 11:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden is giving the 2nd highest civilian award to the leaders of the Jan. 6 congressional committee
item.description: President Joe Biden is bestowing the second highest civilian medal on Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bangladesh court again rejects bail for Hindu leader who led rallies
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A court in southeastern Bangladesh on Thursday rejected a plea for bail by a jailed Hindu leader who led large rallies in the Muslim-majority country demanding better security for minority groups.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Feds probe possible accomplices in NOLA terror attack as thousands prep for Sugar Bowl and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: NYPD says 'not a terrorist attack' after 10 shot outside NYC event space on New Year's Day
item.description: At least 10 people were injured by gunfire late Wednesday at the Amazura nightclub in Jamaica, Queens, New York City police confirmed to Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Firework mortars, gas cannisters stuffed inside Tesla that exploded outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: TY O'NEIL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rescuers find gruesome scene at a Honolulu home after a fireworks blast kills 3, injures over 20
item.description: Emergency crews arrived to a chaotic and gruesome scene in a Honolulu neighborhood after a large New Year's firework tipped over after being lit and ignited a fiery, shrapnel-studded blast that killed three people and injured more than 20 others, several of them critically.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans barriers to prevent vehicle attacks were in the process of being replaced
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Seven years ago, New Orleans officials began installing adjustable barriers at intersections in the famed French Quarter to temporarily prevent vehicles from entering the tourist area where the narrow streets are typically teeming with pedestrians every night.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JEFF MARTIN and KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYE drone show canceled on Folly Beach after drones fall from sky; at least 1 injured
item.description: FOLLY BEACH, S.C. (WCBD) - A new drone show on Folly Beach was canceled Tuesday night after the drones malfunctioned and fell from the sky. Organizers completed two drone displays before they all went down, News 2 learned.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, WGN 9

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — At least 10 people, including two children, were killed and four others were seriously wounded on Wednesday in a shooting rampage that followed a bar brawl in a western Montenegrin city, officials said. The shooter was on the run.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jocelyne Wildenstein, Swiss socialite known for extreme plastic surgeries, dies at 84: reports
item.description: Wildenstein's facial surgeries were notably feline, leading the press to (cruelly) dub her "Catwoman.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: Islamic State-inspired driver expressed desire to kill before deadly New Orleans rampage, Biden says
item.description: An Army veteran driving a pickup truck that bore the flag of the Islamic State group wrought carnage on New Orleans’ raucous New Year’s celebration, killing 15 people as he steered around a police blockade and slammed into revelers before being shot dead by police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: The FBI said it believes it's the largest amount of homemade explosives it has encountered in its history
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Broccoli sold at Walmart stores in 20 states could cause sickness: FDA
item.description: A fresh vegetables and salad company has issued a precautionary advisory for some washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets distributed at Walmart stores in 20 states, including Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family searching for missing Southern California man
item.description: Blackstone’s loved ones have not heard from him since his disappearance and are concerned for his well-being.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he'll attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Asked by The Associated Press as he walked into a New Year's Eve party at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump said "I'll be there.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts condemns elected officials for intimidating judges
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts condemned elected officials who have intimidated judges and defied court rulings, highlighting them in his year-end report Tuesday as part of what Roberts called “illegitimate activity” that threatens the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Excessive screen time leads to more anger, outbursts for preschoolers
item.description: Young children spending more than 75 minutes on a tablet were more likely to show increased outbursts of anger and frustration, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Kate Rix - The 74, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Returning to the office? Here are some tips to find flexibility and help navigate the changes
item.description: Heading into 2025, thousands of workers face an unsettling reality: After years of working from the comfort of home, they must return to the office full-time for the first time since the coronavirus pandemic or look for new work.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US on New Year's Eve
item.description: There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare taught us ways to deny claims: Former employee
item.description: Collins described crying at her desk while handling calls from desperate patients, as supervisors laughed.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How Jimmy Carter's marriage to Rosalynn Carter helped shape his legacy
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter’s marriage to First Lady Rosalynn Carter played a defining role in his life, spanning 77 years and making it the longest presidential marriage in U.S. history. Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4
